page: Google Cloud Platform n1-highmem-8 is a memory-optimized virtual machine belonging to the N1 server family. It features 8 vCPUs mapped to 4 physical cores of an Intel Xeon processor running at 2.3 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. The instance is equipped with 52.0 GB of dedicated system memory, yielding 6.5 GB of RAM per core, but does not include local storage or bundled GPU accelerators.

Performance benchmarks indicate a division between compute-heavy and memory-bound tasks. The server registers weak single-core and multi-core CPU performance in stress-ng and Geekbench tests, alongside poor single-threaded compression and decompression speeds. However, it demonstrates average performance in PassMark CPU tests, database operations, and Redis SET operations, which achieve 5.73 million operations per second. Large-block memory bandwidth read and write speeds also fall into the average tier, though small-block cached read bandwidth remains weak.

From a resource density perspective, this instance offers a high memory-to-vCPU ratio, making it a qualitatively cost-efficient choice for memory-intensive workloads that do not require high-performance compute or local NVMe storage. It is best suited for workloads such as database caching, medium-scale database operations, and memory-bound enterprise applications where memory capacity is prioritized over raw CPU throughput.
description: Google Cloud Platform n1-highmem-8 is a memory-optimized virtual machine from the N1 family, configured with 8 vCPUs, 4 physical Intel Xeon cores running at 2.3 GHz, and 52.0 GB of RAM. It lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. Benchmark data shows weak single-core and multi-core CPU performance alongside poor compression speeds. However, the instance delivers average performance in database operations, Redis SET operations, and large-block memory bandwidth. This hardware profile makes the n1-highmem-8 a qualitatively cost-efficient option for memory-bound workloads, such as database caching and enterprise applications, where memory capacity is more critical than high-speed compute performance.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform n1-highmem-8 is an x86_64 virtual machine featuring 8 vCPUs, 52 GB RAM, and an Intel Xeon CPU, designed for memory-intensive workloads requiring moderate compute.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform n1-highmem-8 features 8 vCPUs and 52 GB RAM, offering average database and memory bandwidth performance for memory-bound workloads.
tagline: A memory-optimized virtual machine featuring 52 GB of RAM and 8 vCPUs for memory-bound enterprise applications and database caching.
bullet_points: Configured with 8 vCPUs and 52.0 GB of system memory
Powered by an Intel Xeon x86_64 processor at 2.3 GHz
Average performance in database operations and Redis SET benchmarks
No bundled local storage or GPU accelerators
Suited for memory-bound workloads and database caching
